directive: Revise thm:sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound to state its chi-square and delta_n expected-length conclusions for every admissible pair, reserving 3s_1 alpha<d solely for the strictly-slower-than-root-n corollary; combine it with lem:parametric-tie-length-lower-bound in thm:honest-adaptation-phase-bracket to state the unified lower scale n^{-1/2} vee n^{-xi}=n^{-min{1/2,xi}} for both frontier criteria. Keep oeq:matching-union-interval and the unresolved portions of oeq:frontier-boundary open rather than marking the loose upper bracket as resolving them. Synchronize the TLDR, project justification, sampling-model description, technical limitation, honest scope, novelty/related-work positioning, and all headline/phase prose so they acknowledge the proved split-sample connected interval while describing only attainment of the sparse lower scale and the remaining phase gaps as open; remove or correct the stale def:union-upper-route dependency of thm:split-sample-union-upper-bracket. Do not add assumptions or claim a matched frontier.

changed.from: For every admissible pair satisfying \(3s_1\alpha<d\), there are fixed admissible choices of \(c_h,c_a,c_N\) and \(\varepsilon\in(0,1-2\gamma)\) such that \(\chi^2(\overline Q_n,(P_0^\circ)^n)\le\exp\{K n^2N_na_n^4h_n^{2d}\}-1\le\varepsilon^2\) eventually.  Hence every \((C_n)_n\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) obeys \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}E_{(P_0^\circ)^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]/\delta_n\ge c_{\gamma,\varepsilon}>0\).  Since \(3s_1\alpha<d\) gives \(\xi<1/2\), union honesty forces a strictly slower-than-root-\(n\) expected length at an exact-tie law even though \(\mathcal P_0\) alone admits \(O(n^{-1/2})\) length.
changed.to: For every admissible pair, there are fixed admissible choices of \(c_h,c_a,c_N\) and \(\varepsilon\in(0,1-2\gamma)\) such that \(\chi^2(\overline Q_n,(P_0^\circ)^n)\le\exp\{K n^2N_na_n^4h_n^{2d}\}-1\le\varepsilon^2\) eventually. Hence every \((C_n)_n\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) obeys \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}E_{(P_0^\circ)^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]/\delta_n\ge c_{\gamma,\varepsilon}>0\). If, in addition, \(3s_1\alpha<d\), then \(\xi<1/2\), so union honesty forces a strictly slower-than-root-\(n\) expected length at an exact-tie law even though \(\mathcal P_0\) alone admits \(O(n^{-1/2})\) length.
changed.reason: The restriction \(3s_1\alpha<d\) is not used in the sparse-mixture membership, chi-square, or interval-length transfer. It is used only to compare \(\delta_n\) with \(n^{-1/2}\). The orchestrator-directed revision therefore separates the universally valid lower bound from its conditional strictly-slower-than-root-\(n\) corollary.

Emit typed core edits/proposed changes only as required by schema, and do not emit a statement-replace on any node whose claim text changes in the same round.
